methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: Native ETH held transiently in THORChain_Router during deposits/outs (msg.value forwarding/bouncing) and permanently if sends fail and is bounced to vault via .transfer()
Native AVAX held transiently in AvaxRouter during deposits/outs (msg.value forwarding/bouncing) and permanently if sends fail and is bounced to vault via .transfer()
ERC-20 tokens custodied by THORChain_Router (any token address != 0) with per-vault internal allowance accounting (_vaultAllowance)
ARC-20 tokens custodied by AvaxRouter (any token address != 0) with per-vault internal allowance accounting (_vaultAllowance)
ETH/WETH liquidity interaction in THORChain_Aggregator and SushiRouterSmol (swapExactTokensForETH, swapExactETHForTokens, WETH.deposit/withdraw)
AVAX/WAVAX liquidity interaction in AvaxAggregator (swapExactTokensForAVAX, swapExactAVAXForTokens)
USDT (TetherToken) balances/totalSupply controlled by owner (issue/redeem/destroyBlackFunds) and fees (basisPointsRate/maximumFee) accruing to owner
ETH.RUNE (ETH_RUNE) ERC20 balances and supply (owner mint up to maxSupply; public burn/burnFrom; nonstandard transferTo using tx.origin)
WETH contract ETH reserves (totalSupply = contract ETH balance) and WETH balances/allowances
Potential unintended token balances trapped in aggregators/routers due to approve patterns and lack of sweeping for non-owner users
state_assets: THORChain_Router._vaultAllowance[vault][asset] (private) accounting for ERC20 custody per vault
AvaxRouter._vaultAllowance[vault][asset] (private) accounting for ARC20 custody per vault
THORChain_Aggregator: WETH address, swapRouter address, owner address, _status reentrancy guard state
AvaxAggregator: WAVAX address, swapRouter address, _status reentrancy guard state
USDT/TetherToken: owner, balances, allowed allowances, basisPointsRate, maximumFee, paused, isBlackListed, _totalSupply, upgradedAddress, deprecated, token metadata
SushiRouterSmol: WETH address constant state, fixed 'one' amount used in swaps
privilege_assets: THORChain_Aggregator.owner: can rescueFunds() any ETH/ERC20 held by aggregator
USDT/TetherToken owner: can pause/unpause, transferOwnership, issue/redeem, setParams, deprecate (forward to upgradedAddress), blacklist and destroyBlackFunds
ETH_RUNE owner: can mint(), transferOwnership(), renounceOwnership()
Implicit privilege in Router design: any address can act as a 'vault' unless offchain governance constrains it; spend authority enforced only by _vaultAllowance keyed by msg.sender
Router-to-router migration surface: transferAllowance/returnVaultAssets can approve external router contracts to pull tokens via depositWithExpiry
reputation_assets: Cross-chain event integrity: Deposit/TransferOut/TransferOutAndCall*/TransferAllowance/VaultTransfer events observed by Bifrost drive cross-chain state; spoofing/parameter confusion harms protocol reputation
Vault trust model: correctness of allowance bookkeeping and outbound transfer authorization affects solvency perception
Aggregator trust: swap execution and fallback behavior impacts user outcomes and protocol credibility
USDT/centralized admin controls and ETH.RUNE tx.origin behavior are governance/reputation sensitive if used in protocol flows

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: THORChain_Router: no inheritance (custom reentrancy guard). AvaxRouter: no inheritance (custom reentrancy guard). THORChain_Aggregator/THORChain_Failing_Aggregator/AvaxAggregator/SushiRouterSmol/WETH: no inheritance. ETH_RUNE: Context -> Ownable; ETH_RUNE implements iERC20 and inherits Context, Ownable. USDT: Ownable base; Pausable is Ownable; BasicToken is Ownable; StandardToken is BasicToken; BlackList is Ownable+BasicToken; TetherToken inherits Pausable, StandardToken, BlackList (multiple inheritance). Test tokens (ERC20Token/EvilERC20Token/StealERC20Token) implement iERC20 and use SafeMath libs; no inheritance.
external_dependencies: External DEX router interface iSWAPROUTER / ISwapRouter (Sushi/Pangolin-like)
External router interface iROUTER/IRouter for cross-router migration via depositWithExpiry
Hardhat console (StealToken) - dev-only dependency; unsafe/unavailable on production chains
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: No proxy/diamond patterns in provided code. USDT includes an application-level 'deprecation' forwarding mechanism (deprecated/upgradedAddress) that routes ERC20 calls to a new contract, but this is not a proxy standard and changes trust assumptions. No UUPS/Transparent/Beacon/Diamond detected.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Routers: no onchain admin; authorization is implicit via msg.sender vault allowance bookkeeping (anyone can become a 'vault' for themselves by receiving allowance via deposits). Aggregators: THORChain_Aggregator and THORChain_Failing_Aggregator have single-owner rescueFunds; all swap functions are permissionless. AvaxAggregator: permissionless, no owner. USDT: centralized owner with pause, blacklist, mint/burn (issue/redeem), parameter setting, and deprecation upgrade switch. ETH_RUNE: centralized owner with mint and ownership transfer/renounce.

state_changes: token approval to external router (if router != this) via safeApprove
external_calls: approve(asset, router, amount) via low-level call (if router != this)
iROUTER(router).depositWithExpiry(vault=newVault, asset, amount, memo, maxuint) (if router != this)
payable(msg.sender).transfer(msg.value) bounce-back on send failure (ETH path)
multiple ETH sends / ERC20 transfers depending on payload
payable(to).send(msg.value) fallback if aggregator call fails
payable(msg.sender).transfer(msg.value) bounce-back if fallback send fails
payable(aggregationPayload.target).send(msg.value) fallback on swap failure (ETH path) [NOTE: sends to target, not recipient]
payable(msg.sender).transfer(msg.value) bounce-back if fallback send fails (ETH path)
fromAsset.transfer(target, fromAmount) via low-level call (ERC20 path)
multiple aggregator target calls and/or ERC20 transfers

state_changes: token approval to external router (if router != this)
external_calls: approve(asset, router, amount) via safeApprove (if router != this)
iROUTER(router).depositWithExpiry(asgard, asset, amount, memo, maxuint) (if router != this)
asset.approve(router, amount) via low-level call (if router != this)
IRouter(router).depositWithExpiry(newVault, asset, amount, memo, maxuint) (if router != this)
payable(msg.sender).transfer(msg.value) bounce-back if fallback send fails
asset.approve(router, amount) via low-level call (if router != this)
IRouter(router).depositWithExpiry(asgard, asset, amount, memo, maxuint) (if router != this)

state_changes: ERC20 approvals to swapRouter (nonzero; not reset to 0 first)
ERC20 approvals to swapRouter (sets 0 then fromAmount in ERC20 branch)
external_calls: fromAsset.approve(swapRouter,0) and approve(swapRouter,fromAmount) via low-level call (ERC20 branch)
address(swapRouter).call(swapExactTokensForETH(...)) (ERC20 branch; relies on msg.sender being aggregator)

state_changes: IERC20(token) allowance to swapRouter set via SafeERC20.safeApprove(amount) (non-zero-to-non-zero may revert for some tokens)
external_calls: tcRouter.depositWithExpiry{value: this.balance}(tcVault, address(0), this.balance, tcMemo, deadline)
delegate/forward call to upgradedAddress.transferByLegacy if deprecated
iROUTER(to).deposit(from, this, value, "") (reentrant-style callback into a router-like contract; first call only)

upgradeability_surface.notes: No proxy. Single deployed implementation. Custom reentrancy guard uses _status storage.
No proxy. Custom reentrancy guard uses _status storage.
No proxy. Owner-controlled rescueFunds introduces admin asset sweep capability.
Testing contract that always reverts in swapOutV5.
No owner/admin. Uses OZ SafeERC20; beware safeApprove non-zero-to-non-zero behavior for some tokens.
Not proxy-based but has application-level upgrade switch (deprecated/upgradedAddress) that forwards ERC20 calls; introduces external trust/call risk and changes semantics without storage migration.
No proxy. Contains nonstandard transferTo using tx.origin.
Canonical single contract; no upgrade hooks.
Test router; fixed output amount; unsafe for production assumptions.
